description: Took over a greenfield frontend with no existing conventions and designed the React architecture from scratch for CyberPlan's next major release. The patterns I established are what the team is building on going forward.

Work directly with product to turn S&OP domain requirements into concrete API and UI decisions. The domain is complex enough that getting the technical model right matters as much as the implementation.
Modernizing development workflows across both legacy and greenfield modules, pulling them toward consistent standards without breaking what's already in production.
Built React applications from zero with no existing standards to follow. The component structure I put in place became the template for the team's subsequent projects.
Introduced Docker across development, QA, and production to fix environment parity issues. QA handoffs stopped being a recurring conversation.
Mentored a junior developer on React architecture; within a few months they were shipping features independently without hand-holding.
Picked up Flutter/FlutterFlow for mobile delivery and wrote simple Python scripts for backend automation in areas where the team had gaps.
Coordinated up to 4 developers across around 10 concurrent projects, hitting ~90% UAT pass rate while managing change requests, release pipelines, and direct end-user relationships in parallel.

Built internal React/Storybook and .NET 6/8 libraries shared across multiple applications. The main payoff was cutting per-project setup time and stopping the team from rebuilding the same components repeatedly.
Designed a full-stack architecture (React + .NET 6/8 + Entity Framework) that the team later adopted as the standard template for all SharePoint Online platform work.
Coordinated a development team of 2–4 developers across multiple concurrent enterprise projects.
Managed production deployment pipelines, software maintenance, bug fixing, change requests, and direct end-user relationships.
Developed business-critical SharePoint Online applications with React, Bootstrap, and jQuery.
Built an Android AR app in Unity that let field workers view 3D ship assembly plans on their phones, no specialist hardware required.

location: On-site, Area Science Park, Trieste, Italy
description: Built an Android app integrated with an existing queue management system. The UI was configurable at runtime so staff could update content without triggering a redeployment.
Built a cross-platform (Android + iOS) patient flow management app for IRCCS Materno Infantile 'Burlo Garofolo' using Angular and NativeScript.
